title: dactylorhiza × aldenii
intro: Dactylorhiza × aldenii, also known as Alden's Orchid, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza incarnata. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza × aldenii include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use in modern medicine is limited due to potential side effects and interactions.
